steps.title: Track the passage order
steps.content: The text first gives specific Muscogee words: hatki and hathaki. It then explains the general phenomenon those words illustrate, reduplication, and ends by saying this type of reduplication is common in Muscogee.

steps.title: Reject absent topics
steps.content: A invents translation issues, B invents scholarly controversy, and C invents a discussion of Muscogee's relationship to other languages. The choice that follows the actual specific-to-general structure of the text is correct. The answer is D.
